dml_fixed.interpretation: epsilon < 0.5 ⇒ DML almost-stochastically dominates vanilla on grad MSE; epsilon close to 0 ⇒ near-complete dominance; epsilon ≥ 0.5 ⇒ no dominance claim (Dror et al. 2019).

dml_gradnorm.interpretation: epsilon < 0.5 ⇒ DML almost-stochastically dominates vanilla on grad MSE; epsilon close to 0 ⇒ near-complete dominance; epsilon ≥ 0.5 ⇒ no dominance claim (Dror et al. 2019).

dml_relobralo.interpretation: epsilon < 0.5 ⇒ DML almost-stochastically dominates vanilla on grad MSE; epsilon close to 0 ⇒ near-complete dominance; epsilon ≥ 0.5 ⇒ no dominance claim (Dror et al. 2019).

dml_warmup.interpretation: epsilon < 0.5 ⇒ DML almost-stochastically dominates vanilla on grad MSE; epsilon close to 0 ⇒ near-complete dominance; epsilon ≥ 0.5 ⇒ no dominance claim (Dror et al. 2019).
